content.text: Tunnel is broken (530 / dead DNS). Disabling and re-enabling it to get a fresh public URL.
Enable timed out. Restarting 9router cleanly, then bringing the tunnel back up.

input.command:
# Check if native module build is stuck / interfering
pgrep -lf 'better-sqlite3|node-gyp|9router' 2>/dev/null
echo '---'
# Kill current 9router stack
pkill -f '/opt/homebrew/bin/9router' 2>/dev/null || true
pkill -f '9router/cli.js' 2>/dev/null || true
pkill -f 'cloudflared tunnel --url http://127.0.0.1:20128' 2>/dev/null || true
# next-server on 20128
PID=$(lsof -nP -iTCP:20128 -sTCP:LISTEN -t 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -n "$PID" ]; then kill $PID 2>/dev/null || true; fi
sleep 2
lsof -nP -iTCP:20128 -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null || echo "port 20128 free"
pgrep -lf '9router|cloudflared tunnel --url http://127.0.0.1:20128' 2>/dev/null || echo "cleared"

# Clean leftovers
pkill -f 'cloudflared tunnel --url http://127.0.0.1:20128' 2>/dev/null || true
kill 55630 55702 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1

# Start 9router fresh
9router --no-browser --skip-update 2>&1

content.text: Tunnel flapped from a race — cleaning up to a single stable cloudflared instance.

input.command:
# Stop ALL racey enable clients and cloudflared children
pkill -f 'api.enableTunnel|getTunnelStatus' 2>/dev/null || true
pkill -f 'cloudflared tunnel --url http://127.0.0.1:20128' 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 2

# Ensure 9router itself is still up
if ! curl -sS --max-time 3 -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://127.0.0.1:20128/ | grep -qE '200|307'; then
  echo "9router down, restarting"
  pkill -f '/opt/homebrew/bin/9router' 2>/dev/null || true
  sleep 1
  nohup 9router --no-browser --skip-update >/tmp/9router-manual.log 2>&1 &
  for i in $(seq 1 15); do
    code=$(curl -sS --max-time 2 -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://127.0.0.1:20128/ 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    [ "$code" = "307" ] || [ "$code" = "200" ] && break
    sleep 1
  done
fi
echo "local=$(curl -sS --max-time 3 -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:20128/)"
pgrep -lf '9router|cloudflared|next-server' | head -20

content.text:
You were right — **9router itself was up**, but the Cloudflare quick tunnel was dead (old hostname no longer resolved; stable alias `rxvudxz.abc-tunnel.us` was returning 530).

I restarted the tunnel. It’s healthy again:

- Local: `http://127.0.0.1:20128` — OK  
- Public: `https://cooler-columnists-them-convention.trycloudflare.com` — 41 models  

**Update Cursor** → Settings → Models → Override OpenAI Base URL to:

`https://cooler-columnists-them-convention.trycloudflare.com/v1`

(same API key as before)

Quick tunnels get a new hostname every restart, which is why this keeps biting you. The short alias still isn’t routing — if you want, we can dig into making that stable next.

input.description: Wait for tunnel registration and retry

content.text: Cloudflared connected then died with a QUIC error. Retrying with HTTP/2.

input.command:
# Clean
pkill -f 'cloudflared tunnel --url http://127.0.0.1:20128' 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 2

CLOUDFLARED=/Users/amin/.9router/bin/cloudflared
rm -f /tmp/9router-tunnel/cf.log

# Prefer http2 to avoid QUIC flake
"$CLOUDFLARED" tunnel --url http://127.0.0.1:20128 --protocol http2 --no-autoupdate --retries 99 \
  > /tmp/9router-tunnel/cf.log 2>&1 &
CF_PID=$!
echo "cf_pid=$CF_PID"

# Wait for URL + registration
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  if rg -q 'Registered tunnel connection' /tmp/9router-tunnel/cf.log 2>/dev/null; then
    break
  fi
  if ! kill -0 $CF_PID 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "died early"; tail -30 /tmp/9router-tunnel/cf.log; exit 1
  fi
  sleep 1
done

URL=$(rg -o 'https://[a-z0-9-]+\.trycloudflare\.com' /tmp/9router-tunnel/cf.log | head -1)
echo "url=$URL"
sleep 2

KEY='REDACTED'
for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do
  code=$(curl -sS --max-time 12 -H "Authorization: Bearer $KEY" "$URL/v1/models" -o /tmp/9r-ok.json -w "%{http_code}" 2>/dev/null || echo fail)
  echo "try $i -> $code"
  if [ "$code" = "200" ]; then
    python3 -c "import json; print(len(json.load(open('/tmp/9r-ok.json'))['data']),'models')"
    # persist
    python3 -c "import json,pathlib; p=pathlib.Path.home()/'.9router/tunnel/state.json'; d=json.loads(p.read_text()) if p.exists() else {}; d['tunnelUrl']='$URL'; p.write_text(json.dumps(d,indent=2)+'\n')"
    ps -p $CF_PID -o pid,etime= && echo STABLE
    exit 0
  fi
  sleep 2
done

echo FAILED
tail -40 /tmp/9router-tunnel/cf.log
ps -p $CF_PID >/dev/null || echo cf_dead
exit 2

pgrep -lf cloudflared; echo '---'; ls -la /tmp/9router-tunnel/; echo '---'; cat /tmp/9router-tunnel/cf.log 2>/dev/null | tail -50; echo '---'; which cloudflared; ls -la /Users/amin/.9router/bin/cloudflared

cat ~/.9router/tunnel/state.json; echo
node <<'EOF'
const api = require('/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/9router/src/cli/api/client.js');
(async () => {
  const st = await api.getTunnelStatus();
  console.log(JSON.stringify(st.data.tunnel, null, 2));
})().catch(e => console.error(e));
EOF

# Find URL from the live cloudflared process config/dir
CFGDIR=$(ps -p 60930 -o args= | rg -o '/var/folders/[^ ]+/cloudflared-quick-[^ ]+' || true)
echo "cfgdir=$CFGDIR"
ls -la "$CFGDIR" 2>/dev/null
# Try to get URL from 9router DB or logs
find ~/.9router -name '*.log' 2>/dev/null | head
rg -o 'https://[a-z0-9-]+\.trycloudflare\.com' ~/.9router /tmp/9router* /var/folders/lx/n08jlv_94hl6r6qj_bnf5p9c0000gn/T/cloudflared-quick-tnWDx7 2>/dev/null | head -20

KEY='REDACTED'
URL=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('/Users/amin/.9router/tunnel/state.json')).get('tunnelUrl') or '')")
echo "state_url=$URL"
if [ -n "$URL" ]; then
  curl -sS --max-time 12 -H "Authorization: Bearer $KEY" "$URL/v1/models" -o /tmp/9r-ok.json -w "public=%{http_code}\n" || true
  python3 -c "import json; print('models', len(json.load(open('/tmp/9r-ok.json'))['data']))" 2>/dev/null || head -c 100 /tmp/9r-ok.json; echo
fi

content.text:
Agreed — `trycloudflare.com` quick tunnels are disposable by design (new hostname every restart, and they die under load). That’s the wrong layer for Cursor.

**Reliable options** (Cursor needs a stable public HTTPS URL; localhost is blocked):

| Option | Stability | Cost / setup |
|--------|-----------|--------------|
| **1. ngrok static domain** | Same URL forever (`*.ngrok-free.dev`) | Free account + authtoken; one Cursor config |
| **2. Named Cloudflare Tunnel** | Same URL forever | CF account + one-time `cloudflared` login |
| **3. Tailscale Funnel** | Stable `*.ts.net` URL; built into 9router | Tailscale account + install |

I’d skip `abc-tunnel.us` — it’s supposed to be 9router’s stable alias, but it’s stuck on **530** even when the quick tunnel is healthy.

**Recommendation:** **ngrok static domain** — fastest path, Cursor community default, configure once.

If you prefer staying inside 9router’s UI, **Tailscale Funnel** is the next best.

Which do you want me to set up? For ngrok I’ll need an authtoken (and the static domain name if you already claimed one).

<timestamp>Sunday, Jul 12, 2026, 10:52 PM (UTC+4)</timestamp>

ok do this
Setting up ngrok with a static domain for 9router. Checking what's already installed.

input.command:
which ngrok; ngrok version 2>/dev/null; ls -la ~/.config/ngrok/ngrok.yml ~/.ngrok2/ngrok.yml 2>/dev/null; brew list ngrok 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; # Don't print secrets - just whether authtoken exists
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
for p in [Path.home()/'.config/ngrok/ngrok.yml', Path.home()/'.ngrok2/ngrok.yml']:
    if p.exists():
        text = p.read_text()
        has_token = 'authtoken:' in text and len([l for l in text.splitlines() if l.strip().startswith('authtoken:') and len(l.split(':',1)[1].strip())>5])>0
        has_domain = 'domain:' in text or 'hostname:' in text
        print(f'{p}: exists token={has_token} domain_cfg={has_domain}')
        # show non-secret keys
        for line in text.splitlines():
            if any(k in line.lower() for k in ['version','region','domain','hostname','addr','proto','tunnels','agent','endpoint']) and 'authtoken' not in line.lower() and 'api_key' not in line.lower():
                print(' ', line)
PY
pgrep -lf 'ngrok' | head -5
curl -sS --max-time 3 http://127.0.0.1:4040/api/tunnels 2>/dev/null | head -c 400; echo
# 9router still up?
curl -sS --max-time 3 -o /dev/null -w "9router=%{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1:20128/

ngrok **3.39.9** is installed, and the flaky Cloudflare quick tunnel is off. 9router is still up locally.

I need your **ngrok authtoken** to finish (and claim a static domain):

1. Open https://dashboard.ngrok.com/get-started/your-authtoken (sign up if needed)
2. Paste the token here

Optional: if you already claimed a static domain under **Domains**, send that hostname too (`something.ngrok-free.dev`). Otherwise I’ll claim/use whatever free static domain the account gets.

# Rewrite config: keep authtoken, pin static domain tunnel

from pathlib import Path
cfg = Path("/Users/amin/Library/Application Support/ngrok/ngrok.yml")

# wait, YAML key with number might need quotes

agent:

tunnels:

    proto: http
    addr: 20128
    url: https://output-violator-phony.ngrok-free.dev
'''
cfg.write_text(new)
print('wrote config (token preserved, domain pinned)')
# verify no accidental print of full token length only
print('token_len', len(token))
for line in cfg.read_text().splitlines():

""" % token

PY

input.description: Start named ngrok tunnel and verify

input.command:
# Create LaunchAgent for persistent ngrok
PLIST="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/com.9router.ngrok.plist"
NGROK=$(which ngrok)

cat > "$PLIST" <<EOF
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>Label</key>
    <string>com.9router.ngrok</string>
    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    <array>
        <string>$NGROK</string>
        <string>start</string>
        <string>ninerouter</string>
        <string>--log</string>
        <string>/tmp/9router-ngrok/ngrok.log</string>
        <string>--log-format</string>
        <string>logfmt</string>
    </array>
    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
    <true/>
    <key>KeepAlive</key>
    <true/>
    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
    <string>/tmp/9router-ngrok/launchd.out.log</string>
    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
    <string>/tmp/9router-ngrok/launchd.err.log</string>
</dict>
</plist>
EOF

mkdir -p /tmp/9router-ngrok

# Don't double-run: unload if present, leave current ngrok running OR switch to launchd
# Current session already has working ngrok (pid 66864). Load KeepAlive would start a second one and fail.
# So: kill current, load launchd which will start it.
launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u)/com.9router.ngrok 2>/dev/null || true
launchctl unload "$PLIST" 2>/dev/null || true
pkill -x ngrok 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 2
launchctl bootstrap gui/$(id -u) "$PLIST" 2>&1 || launchctl load "$PLIST" 2>&1
sleep 4

curl -sS --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:4040/api/tunnels | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print([(t['public_url'], t['config']['addr']) for t in d.get('tunnels',[])])" 2>/dev/null || echo 'api not ready'
KEY='REDACTED'
curl -sS --max-time 15 -H "Authorization: Bearer $KEY" -H "ngrok-skip-browser-warning: true" \
  "https://output-violator-phony.ngrok-free.dev/v1/models" -o /tmp/ngrok-models.json -w "http=%{http_code}\n"
python3 -c "import json; print('models', len(json.load(open('/tmp/ngrok-models.json'))['data']))"
launchctl print gui/$(id -u)/com.9router.ngrok 2>&1 | head -25
pgrep -lf ngrok | head -5
input.description: Install LaunchAgent to keep ngrok alive

Notes:

- 9router still needs to be running locally (`:20128`); ngrok only exposes it.
- You pasted the ngrok authtoken in chat — rotate it in the [ngrok dashboard](https://dashboard.ngrok.com/get-started/your-authtoken) if you want to be safe.

import sys,json
from datetime import datetime
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
reqs=d.get('requests') or d.get('req') or []
# structure may be {uri, requests:[{request:{method,uri,headers},response:{status}}]}
items = d.get('requests', [])
print('count', len(items))
for r in items[:15]:
    req = r.get('request', {})
    res = r.get('response', {})
    method = req.get('method')
    uri = req.get('uri') or req.get('url')
    status = res.get('status') or res.get('status_code')
    ua = ''
    for h in req.get('headers', {}) if isinstance(req.get('headers'), dict) else []:
        pass
    headers = req.get('headers') or {}
    # headers can be dict of lists
    def hget(name):
        for k,v in headers.items():
            if k.lower()==name.lower():
                return v[0] if isinstance(v,list) and v else v
        return ''
    print(f\"{status} {method} {uri} ua={hget('User-Agent')[:60]}\")
" 2>/dev/null || echo 'no request log'

python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('/tmp/ngrok-chat.json'))
if 'error' in d: print('error', d['error'])
else:
  c=d.get('choices',[{}])[0].get('message',{}).get('content','')
  print('reply:', repr(c)[:200])
  print('model:', d.get('model'))
" 2>/dev/null || head -c 400 /tmp/ngrok-chat.json; echo

content.text:
Yes — the stack is working end-to-end:

| Check | Status |
|-------|--------|
| 9router local | up |
| ngrok tunnel | `https://output-violator-phony.ngrok-free.dev` → `:20128` |
| `/v1/models` | 200, 41 models |
| `/v1/chat/completions` | 200, got a streamed reply |

One caveat: ngrok’s request log only shows our probes so far — **no Cursor traffic yet**. So the pipe is good; if chat still fails in Cursor, it’s usually the model name or API key in Settings → Models (must match a 9router model id exactly, e.g. `cc/claude-sonnet-5`).

import sys, json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
items = d.get('requests', [])
print('count', len(items))
for r in items:
    req = r.get('request', {})
    res = r.get('response', {})
    headers = req.get('headers') or {}
    def hget(name):

import json

items=d.get('requests',[])
print('requests', len(items))
for r in items:

        for k,v in headers.items():
            if k.lower()==n.lower():
                return v[0] if isinstance(v,list) and v else v

input.description: Point ngrok at rewrite proxy and verify

content.text:
Found it. Cursor was calling `POST /chat/completions` (no `/v1`) → **404**. That’s the “trouble finding the resource” message.

I added a rewrite proxy in front of 9router so both paths work. Verified: Cursor-style `/chat/completions` now returns **200**.

**In Cursor, check these:**

1. **Override OpenAI Base URL** (either is fine now):
   - `https://output-violator-phony.ngrok-free.dev/v1`
   - or `https://output-violator-phony.ngrok-free.dev`

2. **Custom model id must match 9router exactly**, e.g.:
   - `cc/claude-sonnet-5`
   - not `claude-sonnet-5` / `gpt-4o` (those 404)

3. OpenAI API key = your 9router key, then retry the chat.

If it still fails, tell me which model name you added and I’ll match it to the list.
